TSLA: Ross Gerber Warns Tesla's 'Mad Max' FSD Mode Is 'Basically Unsafe' Following $240 Million Autopilot Liability Verdict. Ross Gerber, co-founder of investment firm Gerber Kawasaki, criticized Tesla's "Mad Max" FSD mode, calling it "basically unsafe" after the company faced a $243 million liability verdict for a fatal Autopilot crash. He expressed concern that the aggressive driving profile, which allows higher speeds and frequent lane changes, could lead to more accidents. Gerber also noted a stagnation in FSD improvements and highlighted increased regulatory and competitive pressure on Tesla's autonomous driving technology.
TGT: Conagra Brands appoints Mulligan and Pietro Satriano to board. Conagra Brands has expanded its board of directors to 12 members by appointing John Mulligan, former COO and CFO of Target Corp, and Pietro Satriano, former Chairman and CEO of US Foods Holding Corp. These appointments aim to strengthen Conagra's focus on innovation, operational efficiency, and retail growth within its North American portfolio. Both new directors bring extensive experience in retail operations, digital transformation, financial oversight, foodservice, supply chain optimization, and corporate governance.

Bealls Inc. taps Ross veteran as VP of stores. Bealls Inc. has appointed David N. Brown, a former regional VP at Ross Dress for Less and district team leader at Target, as its new Vice President of Stores. Brown brings over 25 years of retail experience and will oversee store operations across various regions, with a focus on continuing the company's growth and operational excellence. This strategic hire aims to leverage his expertise in building high-performing teams and executing strategic initiatives as Bealls expands into new markets.

Ross Stores Hits All-Time High Ahead of Q4 Results. Ross Stores (ROST) has reached an all-time high ahead of its Q4 earnings report, with expectations of a strong adjusted profit of $1.90 per share and $6.44 billion in sales, demonstrating resilience in a cautious spending environment. The company has excelled in 2025 due to affordable offerings, strong marketing, and reduced tariff impacts. Analysts from Bernstein and JPMorgan have updated their price targets, indicating a positive outlook for the stock.
SBUX: Starbucks Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day. Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) saw its stock rise by 1.47% to $97.93 on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market which also experienced a strong trading session. Both the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average recorded gains. This marks the second consecutive day of increases for Starbucks' stock.
DIS: Now on Disney+: ABC Local News Feeds From 8 Cities Across the U.S.. Disney+ is expanding its content offering by providing 24/7 ABC local news livestreams from eight major U.S. cities to its subscribers. These streams, featuring local newscasts, breaking news, and weather, are available in the ABC News Hub and Live Hub, and will eventually be prominently featured for subscribers in those markets. This new local news content complements existing national news coverage and the vast entertainment library on Disney+.

Morgan Stanley Conference to Include Disney CFO Hugh Johnston. Hugh Johnston, CFO and Senior Executive Vice President of The Walt Disney Company, will participate in a Q&A session at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 2, 2026. The session will be livestreamed on Disney's investor website. Johnston previously discussed 'higher income' guests as Disney Parks' main consumers, dynamic pricing, and the company's 2026 content budget split.

The Walt Disney Company To Participate In The Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) announced that Hugh Johnston, Senior Executive Vice President and CFO, will participate in a Q&A session at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 2, 2026, at 4:05 p.m. ET. The session will be live-streamed on Disney's investor relations website, where a recording will also be archived.

Disney CFO Hugh Johnston in March 2 Q&A at Morgan Stanley tech-media event. The Walt Disney Company announced that its Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Hugh Johnston, will participate in a question-and-answer session at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 2, 2026. The event will be streamed live online, and a recording will be archived on the company's investor relations website. This engagement provides an opportunity for investors to hear directly from Disney's CFO regarding the company's financial strategies and outlook.
NKE: Nike Tariffs Test Supply Chain Shift And Profitability Story. Nike is facing new 15% global import tariffs from the Trump administration, impacting its heavily international manufacturing, especially in China.
